items.criterion: All 8 existing useShotGroups tests pass without modification
pinnedShotGroups survives full save/load round-trip: serializeForDisk → validateSerializedConfig → buildTimelineData (verified by automated test)
useShotGroups suppresses inferred groups using clipIds subset matching (not shotId:trackId) — verified by new test cases
New TimelineEditMutation variant { type: 'pinnedShotGroups' } compiles and routes correctly in applyEdit
migrateToFlatTracks preserves pinnedShotGroups through both migration paths
render-bounds.validation.test.ts and all other rowsToConfig callers compile with updated signature
npm run build succeeds with no type errors
Switch-to-video snapshots image clips and switch-to-images restores them, each as a single applyEdit call with pinnedShotGroupsOverride
Pinning a suggested group via context menu persists the group in TimelineConfig and it appears with solid border on next render
Unpinning a pinned group removes it from TimelineConfig and it reverts to dashed suggested border (if still inferable)
Undo/redo works for pin, unpin, switch-to-video, and switch-to-images operations (all go through applyEdit)
Dragging any clip in a pinned group moves all group clips together — expansion deferred to after drag threshold, not on pointer-down
Suggested groups render with dashed border; pinned groups render with solid border using existing color-mix pattern (no hardcoded colors)
Pinned groups show shot name label always (not just on hover)
Switch to Video menu item only appears when finalVideoMap.get(shotId) exists; Switch to Images only when mode is 'video'
When clips in a pinned group are deleted, clipIds are updated; empty groups are auto-removed
AI timeline agent (supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/) does not break — verified by code inspection of permissive casting in db.ts
Manual smoke test: create suggested group → pin → switch to video → undo → switch back to images → unpin → verify dashed border returns
Manual smoke test: drag a pinned group across tracks and verify all clips move together

signals.idea:
Implement two-tier shot groups on the video editor timeline: suggested (inferred, dashed border) and pinned (persistent, solid border).

## Core concept
The current useShotGroups inference becomes 'suggested' groups shown with dashed borders. Users can promote them to 'pinned' groups which are stored in timeline config, persist across refresh, and enable advanced features.

## Data model
PinnedShotGroup stored in timelineConfig:
- shotId, trackId, clipIds[], mode: 'images' | 'video', videoAssetKey?, imageClipSnapshot? (for swap-back)

## When pinned groups are created (explicit + implicit):

### Explicit:
1. Right-click a suggested group → 'Pin as Shot Group' - writes PinnedShotGroup to config
2. Drop a shot from shot list/gallery onto timeline - creates pinned group with images expanded as clips

### Implicit (auto-pin):
3. 'Switch to Final Video' on a suggested group - auto-pins first (snapshots image clips), then swaps to video mode
4. When an image is added to a shot that already has a pinned group on the timeline - auto-inserts the new image clip into the pinned group's track at the right position

## Visual distinction:
- Suggested: dashed border, lighter color (current border style → dashed)
- Pinned: solid border, full color (current styling), shot name visible

## Pinned group behavior:
- Clips drag together as a unit (grouped drag)
- Image ↔ video toggle: switch stores snapshot of image clips, restores on switch-back. Single applyEdit each direction.
- Shot image sync: when images are added/removed from a shot, pinned group auto-updates (subscribe to shot changes via realtime or ShotsContext)
- Context menu: Pin/Unpin, Switch to Video/Images, Jump to Shot, Generate Video

## Key architectural decisions:
- pinnedShotGroups stored in TimelineData (persisted via timeline config, undoable)
- useShotGroups still runs for inference but pinned groups take priority (suppress inferred for same shot+track)
- PinnedShotGroup clipIds are the source of truth for which clips belong to the group
- When a pinned group exists, the shot group overlay reads from the pinned data, not from inference

## Key files to research:
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts - current inference
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts - current swap (to be replaced)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts - applyEdit, TimelineEditMutation, timeline config persistence
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts - drag behavior (need grouped drag)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts - drop from outside (need shot drop)
- src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts - TimelineData type, ClipMeta
- src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts - AssetRegistry types
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx - rendering shot groups
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx - context menu
- src/shared/contexts/ShotsContext.tsx - shot data, realtime updates
- src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotImages.ts - shot image data
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts - asset registration
- src/tools/video-editor/contexts/TimelineEditorContext.ts - editor context/providers

resolved_flags.concern: shot-groups: suppressing inferred groups by `shotId + trackId` is too coarse and can hide separate inferred clusters for the same shot on one track.
resolved_flags.resolution:
Revised plan to address all 19 critique flags by incorporating user's answers:

1. **Suppression strategy** (FLAG-001, correctness-1): Changed from shotId:trackId keying to clipIds-based subset matching. Only suppresses an inferred group if its clipIds are a subset of a pinned group's clipIds.

2. **Mutation model** (FLAG-002, correctness-2, callers-1): Added new `{ type: 'pinnedShotGroups' }` mutation variant to TimelineEditMutation for metadata-only changes. Added `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` field to `type: 'rows'` for atomic rows+metadata changes. Does not overload existing `type: 'config'` contract.

3. **Grouped drag timing** (FLAG-003, correctness-3): Deferred group expansion to after drag threshold is crossed. On pointer-down, only record group membership. On first pointermove past threshold, expand draggedClipIds.

4. **Shot drop DnD** (FLAG-004, scope-1, all_locations-3): Expanded Phase 6 to three steps covering shared DnD type definition (`dragDrop.ts`), drag source in shot list, and timeline acceptance (`onTimelineDragOver` + handler + tests). Marked as deferrable follow-up.

5. **migrateToFlatTracks** (FLAG-005, all_locations-1, scope-2): Made explicit as Step 4 — both return paths in `migrate.ts:167-186` must include `pinnedShotGroups`.

6. **Clip lifecycle** (issue_hints-1): Added Step 16 with concrete behavior: deleted clips removed from clipIds, empty groups auto-removed, video clip deletion triggers snapshot restore or group removal.

resolved_flags.concern: timeline-config-mutations: metadata-only pinned-group edits cannot use the current `applyEdit(type='config')` path because that path only accepts `ResolvedTimelineConfig` and serializes only clips/tracks/output/customEffects.
resolved_flags.resolution:
Revised plan to address all 19 critique flags by incorporating user's answers:

1. **Suppression strategy** (FLAG-001, correctness-1): Changed from shotId:trackId keying to clipIds-based subset matching. Only suppresses an inferred group if its clipIds are a subset of a pinned group's clipIds.

2. **Mutation model** (FLAG-002, correctness-2, callers-1): Added new `{ type: 'pinnedShotGroups' }` mutation variant to TimelineEditMutation for metadata-only changes. Added `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` field to `type: 'rows'` for atomic rows+metadata changes. Does not overload existing `type: 'config'` contract.

3. **Grouped drag timing** (FLAG-003, correctness-3): Deferred group expansion to after drag threshold is crossed. On pointer-down, only record group membership. On first pointermove past threshold, expand draggedClipIds.

4. **Shot drop DnD** (FLAG-004, scope-1, all_locations-3): Expanded Phase 6 to three steps covering shared DnD type definition (`dragDrop.ts`), drag source in shot list, and timeline acceptance (`onTimelineDragOver` + handler + tests). Marked as deferrable follow-up.

5. **migrateToFlatTracks** (FLAG-005, all_locations-1, scope-2): Made explicit as Step 4 — both return paths in `migrate.ts:167-186` must include `pinnedShotGroups`.

6. **Clip lifecycle** (issue_hints-1): Added Step 16 with concrete behavior: deleted clips removed from clipIds, empty groups auto-removed, video clip deletion triggers snapshot restore or group removal.

7. **hasFinalVideo guard** (issue_hints-2, callers-2): Preserved in Step 13 — Switch to Video only shows when `hasFinalVideo`, Switch to Images only when `mode === 'video'`. Preserved callback signature and dismissal flow.

8. **rowsToConfig callers** (all_locations-2): Added Step 7 to update `render-bounds.validation.test.ts:125` and any other direct callers.

9. **AI timeline agent** (criteria_quality-2): Verified in Step 8 — agent uses permissive `as TimelineConfig` cast, no field validation, extra keys pass through.

resolved_flags.concern: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?: The current editor state model funnels mutations through immutable `applyEdit()` calls that rebuild `TimelineData` from configs or rows. The plan's alternate idea of updating `pinnedShotGroups` on `dataRef.current.config` before calling `applyEdit()` cuts across that convention and would make undo/history reasoning much harder.
resolved_flags.resolution: The current editor state model funnels mutations through immutable `applyEdit()` calls that rebuild `TimelineData` from configs or rows. The plan's alternate idea of updating `pinnedShotGroups` on `dataRef.current.config` before calling `applyEdit()` cuts across that convention and would make undo/history reasoning much harder.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Several `must` criteria are user-visible behaviors with weak automated evidence in the plan, including the solid/dashed border rendering, undo/redo behavior for pin and switch operations, and grouped drag moving all clips together. Those are important goals, but without matching automated checks they are not strong review-gate `must` criteria as written.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 31.0 -> 34.25. Plan deltas: 53.6%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 8. Open significant flags: 22.

debt_overlaps.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the revised plan against the original feature brief. If shot drop and auto-sync remain deferrable, the shipped work becomes a narrower 'pin inferred groups and toggle video/images' feature rather than the full requested two-tier system with creation and synchronization flows, so the broader scope tension is still present.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: if phases 6 and 7 are deferred, the shipped work becomes a narrower "pin existing inferred groups and toggle them" feature rather than the full brief, which also called out shot-drop creation and shot-image auto-sync. that broader scope tension is still present in the revised plan.

debt_overlaps.concern: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked the supporting test infrastructure already present in the repo. The validation section still does not add focused tests for the new `type: 'pinnedShotGroups'` mutation, the `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` rows path, or history/save behavior around those edits, even though `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelineHistory`, and `timeline-save-utils.test.ts` are the exact layers where regressions would surface.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: the supporting test infrastructure remains under-specified for the new mutation branch. `usetimelinecommit`, `timeline-save-utils.test.ts`, and `usetimelinehistory` all exercise config rebuild behavior today, but the validation section still does not add focused tests for the new `type: 'pinnedshotgroups'` path or for `pinnedshotgroupsoverride` on rows edits.

debt_overlaps.concern: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the asset-drop callers the plan says to reuse. `handleAssetDrop()` in `useAssetManagement` immediately mutates state and calls `applyEdit()` per asset, and `dispatchTimelineDrop()` in `useExternalDrop` is built around that imperative helper, so the plan cannot literally reuse those callers for an atomic multi-clip shot drop without first extracting a lower-level pure builder.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: find the callers of the changed function. what arguments do they actually pass? does the fix handle all of them?: `handleassetdrop()` and the external-drop wrapper are not pure builders; they immediately mutate editor state and call `applyedit()` per asset. that means the plan cannot literally reuse those callers for atomic shot-drop creation without first extracting a lower-level helper, or it will violate its own "single applyedit" requirement.

debt_overlaps.concern: Media insertion conventions: the plan still says to reuse asset-management helpers for shot drop, but the current helpers are side-effectful and commit immediately, so atomic shot-drop creation still needs a lower-level extracted helper rather than direct reuse.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: media insertion conventions: the plan still says to reuse asset-management helpers for shot drop, but the current helpers are side-effectful and commit immediately, so atomic shot-drop creation still needs a lower-level extracted helper rather than direct reuse.

debt_overlaps.concern: Timeline signatures: applying `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` after `materializeData()` would leave `stableSignature` and other config-derived state stale unless the code rebuilds `TimelineData` from the updated config.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: timeline signatures: applying `pinnedshotgroupsoverride` after `materializedata()` would leave `stablesignature` and other config-derived state stale unless the code rebuilds `timelinedata` from the updated config.

debt_overlaps.concern: Undo reconciliation: Step 16's post-commit `reconcilePinnedGroups` would create extra save/history events for a single delete unless it is explicitly coalesced with the triggering edit or marked to skip history.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: undo reconciliation: step 16's post-commit `reconcilepinnedgroups` would create extra save/history events for a single delete unless it is explicitly coalesced with the triggering edit or marked to skip history.

debt_overlaps.concern: Pinned-group track sync: cross-track drag/move paths must also update `PinnedShotGroup.trackId`, but the revised plan only discusses expanding `draggedClipIds` and does not call out metadata updates for the move commits.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: pinned-group track sync: cross-track drag/move paths must also update `pinnedshotgroup.trackid`, but the revised plan only discusses expanding `draggedclipids` and does not call out metadata updates for the move commits.

debt_overlaps.concern: Scope management: marking shot drop and auto-sync as deferrable follow-ups narrows the deliverable below the original task brief unless the split is explicitly approved.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: scope management: marking shot drop and auto-sync as deferrable follow-ups narrows the deliverable below the original task brief unless the split is explicitly approved.

debt_overlaps.concern: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: One `must` criterion is still an implementation-detail proxy rather than a feature outcome: 'New TimelineEditMutation variant { type: "pinnedShotGroups" } compiles and routes correctly in applyEdit.' That is verifiable, but it does not directly prove the user-visible persistence, undo, or synchronization behavior the feature actually needs.
debt_overlaps.debt_concern: are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: one `must` criterion is still an implementation-detail proxy rather than an outcome: "new timelineeditmutation variant { type: 'pinnedshotgroups' } compiles and routes correctly in applyedit." it is verifiable, but it measures an internal mechanism rather than the user-facing persistence/undo behavior the feature actually needs.
